description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a steeply pitched gable roof clad in reddish-brown panels. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and traditional appearance. The front facade features a large sliding barn door and a smaller adjacent door, both reinforced with visible black metal hinges, as well as an open bay supported by wooden posts for easy livestock access. The side and rear walls are solid, with no visible windows or openings, emphasizing its utilitarian function. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shelter at the entrance and along the sides. The overall structure is robust and practical, designed to house cattle and withstand rural environmental conditions.
description: A traditional wooden cowshed with a steep red gable roof, large sliding barn doors, and an open bay for livestock access. The structure is solid and utilitarian, with no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large sliding barn door, a smaller adjacent door, and an open bay supported by posts under the overhanging roof.
description_view_1: Gable end view displaying the triangular roof profile and solid wooden wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a continuous solid wooden wall and the full expanse of the red gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end view, similar to view 1, with a solid wall and the steep roof profile.
attribute_door_style: sliding barn door and single wooden plank door with metal hinges
